subject: [zzzzteana] Dracula theme park could be switched to Bucharest
body:
Ananova:
Dracula theme park could be switched to Bucharest

A controversial scheme to build a Dracula theme park in Romania could be
switched away from Transylvania.
Consultants PricewaterhouseCoopers is now recommending that it be built in
Bucharest instead.
It comes after Prince Charles led international protests against the
original proposals to build it in the medieval town of Sigishoara.
PricewaterhouseCoopers name Bucharest, originally believed to be an outsider
in the race to host the park, as the most profitable location for the
project.
But that has angered residents in Sigishoara, where Vlad the Impaler, the
inspiration for Dracula, was born. They are counting on the park to boost
the local economy.
Evenimentul Zilei reports that Sigishoara was placed behind the capital with
the Black Sea port of Constanta third choice.
Dorin Danesan, mayor of Sigishoara, said: "The pre-feasibility report from
PWC shows that the park would attract more tourists if it was located in
Bucharest. But I still think that Sigishoara is the best location for it."
The park would include hotels, a Dracula roller coaster, catacombs, a ghost
train and a house of horrors, as well as vampire dungeons located around a
reconstruction of Dracula's castle and an artificial lake.
The project has also been objected to by the world heritage organisation
Unesco fearing Sigishoara might be spoilt.
PricewaterhouseCoopers is to present its final report on the project on
October 15.

Dear Cardholder

We attach your card e-statement, which is password-protected to ensure confidentiality. To view your e-statement you'll need Adobe Reader, which you can download from adobe.com if you don't already have it.

The password to open the document is the identity or password number you used to open your account.

If you have any questions, call us on 0800 555 111 or 011 710 4710.

For more information on your credit card benefits or other Nedbank products, visit nedbank.co.za.

Kind regards
The Nedbank Team

How to verify the integrity and origin of this email

1 Check whether the message has been signed by the sender.
2 Be on the lookout for a security warning when opening this email to alert you that the message might have been tampered with, or that it might not come from the supposed sender.
3 Simply click on the red ribbon icon and choose the option to view the digital identity or the certificate to verify the sender's digital identity.

subject: Netflix Cracks Down
body: Forbes NewslettersPlus: First Republic’s Wealth Advisors Voted With Their Feet–And It Wasn’t For JPMorganADVERTISEMENTBECOME A MEMBER • Newsletters • MyForbesGood morning, For any recent graduates out there, Forbes just released its annual list of America’s Best Employers for New Grads. We built the list by asking more than 28,000 young and early career professionals what they thought of their employers — from basics like salary and benefits as well as values-based parameters like career advancement, DEI initiatives and work-life balance.General Mills, NASA and Google took the top three spots, here’s the full list. Sarah Whitmire Senior Editor, Newsletters Follow me on Twitter and Forbes.comBREAKING NEWSGETTY IMAGESFlorida Gov. Ron DeSantis is expected to announce his 2024 campaign for president later today, as part of a Twitter Spaces event with CEO Elon Musk. He’ll enter the race for the GOP nomination after months of speculation, and the two-term Florida governor is slumping in hypothetical polls against former President Donald Trump.One of Mexico’s largest active volcanoes began erupting last week and officials warned mass evacuations could be coming to the area. Popocatépetl is 45 miles from Mexico City, and more than 25 million people live within 60 miles, where plumes of smoke and ash have already prompted nearby airports to temporarily close. BUSINESS + FINANCEMeta was forced to sell online GIF library Giphy to Shutterstock at a $262 million loss, after a prolonged legal battle with the U.K.’s antitrust watchdog. Shutterstock paid $53 million in net cash for Giphy, three years after Facebook bought the platform for $315 million.TECH + INNOVATIONNURPHOTO VIA GETTY IMAGESMicrosoft said it will integrate its Bing search engine into ChatGPT for all users, expanding the chatbot’s capability to provide real-time information. A previous model available only to subscribers also used Bing, although it was not disclosed. Currently ChatGPT is limited to data from before September 2021.Nvidia’s upcoming quarterly financial report is expected to be a good one, showing its investments in AI and metaverse software subscriptions are paying off, offsetting much of the chipmaker’s loss of semiconductor sales related to ether mining. The Ethereum network’s shift to an energy-efficient business model obliterated most of the company’s income from graphic processing units.Adobe is bringing AI to Photoshop by way of a new tool called Firefly. It will work similarly to other AI image software like DALL-E by allowing people to “add, extend, or remove content” from images using text prompts. Any AI-altered images will be labeled with metadata in an effort to increase transparency.SPORTS + ENTERTAINMENTAssociated PressNetflix announced it would finally begin its widely-anticipated crackdown on password sharing in the U.S., telling users an “account is for use by one household.” The streaming service is one of the first to disallow password sharing; as of February, Netflix said 100 million households share their passwords.TRAVEL + LIFESTYLEFord will include AM radio in all its 2024 Ford and Lincoln vehicles after all, a reversal from previous plans after pushback from lawmakers and radio pundits. While listeners have found alternatives in streaming, as well as FM and satellite radio, AM remains crucial for public emergency broadcasts. Separately, conservatives were concerned about potential impact on political talk shows, which largely appear on the AM band.TRENDS + EXPLAINERSAmong his many plans, Twitter owner Elon Musk has said he wants to turn the social media platform into an “everything app”—and there’s a good blueprint for that concept in Tencent’s WeChat. It has 1.3 billion monthly active users, mainly in China, and has evolved from a simple messaging app when it launched in 2011 to a super app where people can make payments, book flight tickets, play games and even hail a cab. Daily Cover Story ILLUSTRATION BY ANGELICA ALZONA FOR FORBESFirst Republic Wealth Advisors Voted With Their Feet–And It Wasn’t For JPMorganRead ArticleTOPLINEIn the lead-up to First Republic’s collapse, depositors and shareholders weren’t the only ones fleeing—the bank’s wealth-management advisors also headed for the exits. More than 40% of First Republic Bank advisors left between the end of February and May 15, FINRA data show.One place they didn’t go en masse? JPMorgan Chase, the nation’s largest bank, which on May 1 bought most of First Republic’s assets, including the wealth management operation, for $10.6 billion in a transaction arranged by the FDIC.Analysts and JPMorgan have touted First Republic’s $290 billion in wealth management assets as a selling point for the deal. But the advisor exodus suggests that the big bank may retain fewer of those assets than widely believed, since wealthy investors tend to follow advisors when they jump ship. Of the 152 advisors we identified as leaving during the time period we targeted, 28 went to Rockefeller Capital Management and 19 went to Royal Bank of Canada’s wealth management arm. But the big winner was Morgan Stanley, which picked up 49. Only 11 First Republic advisors had moved to JPMorgan before the deal, according to registration filings. It remains to be seen how many of the First Republic advisors who haven’t left already (220, according to JPMorgan’s most recent official estimate) will stay at the bank for the long run. WHY IT MATTERSJPMorgan surely realized that such an exodus was taking place, and adjusted its bid accordingly. Filings show that just acquiring First Republic’s commercial banking business, which accounted for 82% of the bank’s more than $1.6 billion in net income last year, would leave JPMorgan with good returns, according to Eric Compton, a senior analyst at Morningstar. “Any ability to keep any of the additional value of First Republic (wealth clients, wealth advisors, private bankers, etc.) would be extra optionality on top of that, which is the smart way to do it,” he says. subject: [UAI] 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems
body: FINAL Call for papers - 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications (AISTA2004) **** Please apologize for multiple copies **** FINAL Call for Papers AISTA 2004 --> 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications November 15-18, 2004 --> Centre de Recherche Public Henri Tudor, Luxembourg-Kirchberg, Luxembourg http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004 IMPORTANT DATES: Submission of extended summaries: September 15, 2004 Notification of acceptance: September 26, 2004 Camera-ready paper due: October 24, 2004 AISTA Conference: November 15-18, 2004 AIMS and SCOPE: The International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications - AISTA'2004 aims at bringing together theoreticians and practitioners of Intelligent Systems from Luxembourg and around the world to exchange ideas, present their recent results and discuss possible collaborations. The focus of the conference includes, but is not limited=20 to the areas listed below. * Control Systems: Neural Networks Control, Fuzzy Logic Control, Genetic Algorithms & Evolutionary Control, Model-Predictive Control, Adaptive and Optimal Control, Intelligent Control Systems, Robotics and Automation, Fault Diagnosis, Intelligent agents & Multiagent Systems, Industrial Automations Hybrid Systems; Fuzzy Evolutionary Systems, Fuzzy Expert Systems, Fuzzy Neural Systems, Neural Genetic Systems, Neural-Fuzzy-Genetic Systems, Hybrid Systems for Optimization. * Optimization and Prediction: Multi-criteria Optimization, Signal Processing, Time Series Analysis & Prediction, System Identification, Data Mining & Knowledge Discovery, Intelligent Information Systems, Image Processing, Parallel Computing applications in Identification & Control, Pattern Recognition, Clustering, Classification. * Decision Making: Intelligent Databases & Information Retrieval, Case-Based Reasoning, Decision Analysis, Dynamic Systems Modeling, Decision Support Systems, Qualitative and Approximate-Reasoning Modeling. * Security: Intrusion Detection, Privilege Determining, Denial-of-Service Resistance, Attack Resistance, Cryptographic Mechanisms and Protocols, Mobile Environment Security, Wireless Security, Access control, Database=20 Security, Identification and Authentication. * Workshop: A workshop dedicated to Satellite Control and Communications will take place in parallel to the conference sessions. Topics might come for=20 example from the field of Intelligent Ground Control Systems, Autonomous Satellite Cluster Control, Intelligent Telemetry Processing, and Intelligent Antenna Systems. SUBMISSION OF PAPERS Papers reporting original and unpublished research or application results pertaining to the above and related topics are solicited. Papers will be reviewed on the basis of two pages extended abstracts of sufficient detail to permit reasonable evaluation. Accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings with an ISBN. The final version of accepted papers should be of six pages long. Submissions should include the title, author(s), affiliation(s), e-mail address(es), tel/fax numbers, postal address(es) and keywords on the first page. Papers should be submitted on the conference web site: http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004. If web=20 submission is not possible, manuscripts should be sent as an attachment via email to one of the Program Chairs listed below on before the deadline date of September 5, 2004. A special issue of the international of Integrated=20 Computer-Aided Engineering (ICAE) will be devoted to publish extended versions of the=20 best papers=20 presented at AISTA'04. subject: Encountered Login Issue
body:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am experiencing an inexplicable login problem that has suddenly arisen while attempting to access my account. The error message did not offer any specific information, but I believe it may be connected to the recent security enhancements.

I have already taken the step of restarting my browser and clearing the cache, yet the issue remains unresolved. I would be extremely grateful if you could examine this issue and provide a resolution at your earliest convenience.

Here are the key points of the problem:
- Happened: Unexpected login issue occurred suddenly.
- Why: Possibly attributed to recent security updates.
- Tried: Restarted browser and cleared cache already.

I kindly request your assistance in resolving this matter. Could you please investigate and provide a solution or alternative method to restore access to my account? Any advice or assistance you can provide would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your time and cooperation.
I look forward to your prompt response.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

subject: Reactivate Your Amazon Account!
body: Dear member, We regret to inform you that your Amazon account is suspended for a 3-4 days period and then will be terminated. While doing out regularly schedule account verification we have noticed a slight error in your billing information. This might be due to either following reasons: * A recent change in your personal information (change of address) * Submitting invalid information during the initial sign up process * An inability to accurately verify your selected option of payment. Please sign in and reactivate your account: Best Regards , NOTE : This message was sent to you by an automated e-mail system. Do not reply!
model_reason: Suspicious email from an automated system, asking for account reactivation with a sense of urgency and potential threat of account termination.
